LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFF ANDREW SMITH'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 3.1324(B) PROVIDES THAT A DECLARATION MUST ACCOMPANY A MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND. CCP SEC. 2105.5 PROVIDES THAT A DECLARATION MUST EITHER STATE THAT THE DECLARATION WAS EXECUTED IN CALIFORNIA OR STATE THAT THE DECLARATION WAS MADE UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY UNDER THE LAWS OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA. THIS STATEMENT IS MANDATORY IN ORDER FOR A DECLARATION TO BE ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE. (KULSHRESTHA V. FIRST UNION COMMERCIAL CORP. (2004) 33 CAL.4TH 601, 611-12.) PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION FAILS TO COMPLY WITH CCP SEC. 2105.5. BECAUSE THE DECLARATION IS INADMISSIBLE, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION DOES NOT COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1324(B) AND IS DENIED. ALL REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: RICHARD A. KRAMER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976. (302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS THE OPERA PLAZA RESIDENTIAL PARCEL HOMEOWNERS AND THE OPERA PLAZA MASTER OWNER'S ASSOCIATION'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART, AND OVERRULED IN PART. AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT ("SAC") ADEQUATELY ALLEGES THE ELEMENTS OF BREACH OF CONTRACT - (1) PLAINTIFF ENTERED INTO AN AGREEMENT WITH DEFENDANTS (SAC, 7-8); (2) PER THE TERMS OF THE AGREEMENT, THE BOARD MUST NOTICE AN OWNER IN WRITING IF DISCIPLINARY ACTION IS TO BE TAKEN AGAINST HIM (SAC, 14-15); (3) THE BOARD NEVER NOTIFIED PLAINTIFF THAT HE WOULD BE DENIED ACCESS TO THE PARKING GARAGE (SAC, 23); AND (4) PLAINTIFF WAS DAMAGED BY THIS BREACH. (SAC, 24.) AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. THE SAC PLEADS AROUND THE BUSINESS JUDGMENT RULE BY ALLEGING THAT DEFENDANTS ACTED "TO INCREASE REVENUES BY 25% MONTHLY." (SAC, 30; SEE EVEREST INVESTORS 8 V. MCNEIL PARTNERS (2003) 114 CAL.APP.4TH 411, 430, "THE BUSINESS JUDGMENT RULE DOES NOT SHIELD ACTIONS TAKEN WITHOUT REASONABLE INQUIRY, WITH IMPROPER MOTIVES, OR AS A RESULT OF A CONFLICT OF INTEREST.") AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR TRESPASS, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE SAC DOES NOT PLEAD FACTS THAT ESTABLISH THAT DEFENDANTS ENTERED PROPERTY POSSESSED OR CONTROLLED BY PLAINTIFF. (SEE CIVIC WESTERN CORP. V. ZILA INDUSTRIES, INC. (1977) 66 CAL.APP.3D 1, 16, "THE ESSENCE OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR TRESPASS IS AN 'UNAUTHORIZED ENTRY' ONTO THE LAND OF ANOTHER.") THE DEMURRER TO THIS CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND IN LIGHT OF THE FACT THAT PLAINTIFF HAS ALREADY HAD TWO OPPORTUNITIES TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR5976

THE MOTION TO STRIKE THE ADDITIONAL ALLEGATIONS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFF EXCEEDED THE SCOPE OF THE ORDER GRANTING HIM LEAVE TO AMEND, GIVEN THE LIBERAL POLICY OF AMENDMENTS TO PLEADINGS, THE COURT FINDS THAT IT SERVES JUDICIAL ECONOMY TO ALLOW THE ALLEGATIONS TO REMAIN IN THE COMPLAINT, RATHER THAN HAVE PLAINTIFF FILE A MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND, WHICH WOULD MORE LIKELY THAN NOT BE GRANTED. THE MOTION TO STRIKE THE PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS GRANTED, AS THE SAC DOES NOT ALLEGE THAT DEFENDANTS ENGAGED IN MALICIOUS OR FRAUDULENT CONDUCT. (SEE BROUSSEA V. JARRETT (1977) 73 CAL.APP.3RD 864.) PARAGRAPHS 98 AND 100 ARE STRICKEN FROM THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. LAW AND MOTION, 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT ANDREW SMITH'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT - THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. SECTION 7.2 OF THE PARTIES' CC&RS IS AN INDEMNITY CLAUSE, NOT AN UNENFORCEABLE EXCULPATORY CLAUSE. "A CLAUSE WHICH CONTAINS THE WORDS 'INDEMNIFY' AND 'HOLD HARMLESS' IS AN INDEMNITY CLAUSE WHICH GENERALLY OBLIGATES THE INDEMNITOR TO REIMBURSE THE INDEMNITEE FOR ANY DAMAGES THE INDEMNITEE BECOMES OBLIGATED TO PAY THIRD PERSONS. [CITATION.] INDEMNIFICATION AGREEMENTS ORDINARILY RELATE TO THIRD PARTY CLAIMS." MYERS BUILDING INDUSTRIES, LTD. V. INTERFACE TECHNOLOGY, INC. (1993) 13 CAL.APP.4TH 949, 969. PLAINTIFF'S REMAINING ARGUMENTS RELATE TO FACTUAL ISSUES THAT CANNOT BE RESOLVED ON DEMURRER. THE COURT DENIES DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS KATHLEEN CALLINAN AND OPERA PLAZA, LP'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION WAS UNTIMELY, AS IT WAS ONLY FILED ONE DAY BEFORE DEFENDANTS' REPLY BRIEF WAS DUE. (SEE CCP SEC. 1005(B).) EVEN CONSIDERING THE LATE-FILED OPPOSITION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED ON THE MERITS. AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT, THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT ("SAC") DOES NOT PLEAD FACTS TO ESTABLISH THAT PLAINTIFF WAS A PARTY TO THE CONTRACT. AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT, THE SAC DOES NOT PLEAD FACTS TO ESTABLISH THAT PLAINTIFF WAS A PARTY TO THE CONTRACT. ADDITIONALLY, THE SAC DOES NOT PLEAD FACTS THAT ESTABLISH THAT THE CONTRACT WAS EXPRESSLY ENTERED INTO FOR THE BENEFIT OF PLAINTIFF; THUS, PLAINTIFF CANNOT ENFORCE THE CONTRACT UNDER A THIRD PARTY BENEFICIARY THEORY. (SEE SHUTES V. CHENEY (1954) 123 CAL.APP.2D 256, 262.) FURTHER, PLAINTIFF PROVIDES NO LEGAL SUPPORT FOR HIS ASSERTION IN HIS OPPOSITION BRIEF THAT "[A]S A DUES PAYING HOMEOWNER, PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO ENFORCE THE GENERAL CONTRACT'" (OPPOSITION, P. 3.) AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY, THE SAC DOES NOT PLEAD FACTS THAT ESTABLISH DEFENDANTS OWED A FIDUCIARY DUTY TO PLAINTIFFS. AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR TRESPASS TO REAL PROPERTY, THE SAC DOES NOT PLEAD FACTS THAT ESTABLISH THAT DEFENDANTS ENTERED PROPERTY POSSESSED OR CONTROLLED BY PLAINTIFF. (SEE CIVIC WESTERN CORP. V. ZILA INDUSTRIES, INC. (1977) 66 CAL.APP.3D 1, 16, "THE ESSENCE OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR TRESPASS IS AN 'UNAUTHORIZED ENTRY' ONTO THE LAND OF ANOTHER.") THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND IN LIGHT OF THE FACT THAT PLAINTIFF HAS ALREADY HAD TWO OPPORTUNITIES TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-17-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
CROSS-DEFENDANT ANDREW SMITH's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT - The demurrer is overruled. Section 7.2 of the parties' CC&Rs is an indemnity clause, not an unenforceable exculpatory clause. "A clause which contains the words 'indemnify' and 'hold harmless' is an indemnity clause which generally obligates the indemnitor to reimburse the indemnitee for any damages the indemnitee becomes obligated to pay third persons. [Citation.] Indemnification agreements ordinarily relate to third party claims." Myers Building Industries, Ltd. v. Interface Technology, Inc. (1993) 13 Cal.App.4th 949, 969. Plaintiff's remaining arguments relate to factual issues that cannot be resolved on demurrer. The court denies defendants' request for sanctions. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/MJM)

Motion For Leave To Amend The Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-24-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Andrew Smith, Tel. 415.203.2782, plaintiff and moving party appearing on his own behalf;
R. Jason Honey, Esq. of Ericksen Arbuthnot, Tel. 415.362.7126, for defendants and opposing party;
Sharon B. Futerman, Esq. of Prout LeVangie, Tel. 916.443.4849, for defendants and opposing party, by Court Call.

RULING: The matter was not argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF ANDREW SMITH'S Motion For Leave To Amend The Second Amended Complaint is Denied. California Rule of Court 3.1324(b) provides that a declaration must accompany a motion for leave to amend. CCP sec. 2105.5 provides that a declaration must either state that the declaration was executed in California or state that the declaration was made under penalty of perjury under the laws of the State of California. This statement is mandatory in order for a declaration to be admitted into evidence. (Kulshrestha v. First Union Commercial Corp. (2004) 33 Cal.4th 601, 611-12.) Plaintiff's declaration fails to comply with CCP sec. 2105.5. Because the declaration is inadmissible, Plaintiff's motion does not comply with CRC 3.1324(b) and is denied. All requests for sanctions are denied. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Richard A. Kramer; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

Off Calendar Jan-22-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ANDREW SMITH, 415.203.2782, PLAINTIFF IN PROPRIA PERSONA.
ALBERT FINCH, ESQ., 415.362.7126, FOR DEFENDANTS KATHLEEN CALLINAN AND OPERA PLAZA, LP.

RULING: No argument. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs THE OPERA PLAZA RESIDENTIAL PARCEL HOMEOWNERS and THE OPERA PLAZA MASTER OWNER'S ASSOCIATION ("MOA") MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied in part and granted in part. The motion to strike the additional allegations to the third cause of action is denied. Although Plaintiff exceeded the scope of the order granting him leave to amend, given the liberal policy of amendments to pleadings, the Court finds that it serves judicial economy to allow the allegations to remain in the complaint, rather than have Plaintiff file a motion for leave to amend, which would more likely than not be granted. The motion to strike the prayer for punitive damages is granted, as the SAC does not allege that Defendants engaged in malicious or fraudulent conduct. (See Broussea v. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-22-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ANDREW SMITH, 415.203.2782, PLAINTIFF IN PROPRIA PERSONA.
ALBERT FINCH, ESQ., 415.362.7126, FOR DEFENDANTS KATHLEEN CALLINAN AND OPERA PLAZA, LP.
SHARON FUTERMAN, ESQ., 916.443.4849, FOR DEFENDANTS THE OPERA PLAZA HOA AND THE OPERA PLAZA HOA.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTs THE OPERA PLAZA RESIDENTIAL PARCEL HOMEOWNERS and THE OPERA PLAZA MASTER OWNER'S ASSOCIATION's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained in part, and overruled in part. As to the first cause of action for breach of contract, the demurrer is overruled. The Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") adequately alleges the elements of breach of contract - (1) Plaintiff entered into an agreement with Defendants (SAC, 7-8); (2) per the terms of the agreement, the Board must notice an owner in writing if disciplinary action is to be taken against him (SAC, 14-15); (3) the Board never notified Plaintiff that he would be denied access to the parking garage (SAC, 23); and (4) Plaintiff was damaged by this breach. (SAC, 24.) As to the third cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty, the demurrer is overruled. The SAC pleads around the business judgment rule by alleging that Defendants acted "to increase revenues by 25% monthly." (SAC, 30; see Everest Investors 8 v. McNeil Partners (2003) 114 Cal.App.4th 411, 430, "The business judgment rule does not shield actions taken without reasonable inquiry, with improper motives, or as a result of a conflict of interest.") As to the fourth cause of action for trespass, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The SAC does not plead facts that establish that Defendants entered property possessed or controlled by Plaintiff. (See Civic Western Corp. v. Zila Industries, Inc. (1977) 66 Cal.App.3d 1, 16, "The essence of the cause of action for trespass is an 'unauthorized entry' onto the land of another.") The demurrer to this cause of action is sustained without leave to amend in light of the fact that Plaintiff has already had two opportunities to amend the complaint to state a cause of action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-22-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ANDREW SMITH, 415.203.2782, PLAINTIFF IN PROPRIA PERSONA.
ALBERT FINCH, ESQ., 415.362.7126, FOR DEFENDANTS KATHLEEN CALLINAN AND OPERA PLAZA, LP.
SHARON FUTERMAN, ESQ., 916.443.4849, FOR DEFENDANTS THE OPERA PLAZA HOA AND THE OPERA PLAZA HOA.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTs KATHLEEN CALLINAN and OPERA PLAZA, LP'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff's opposition was untimely, as it was only filed one day before Defendants' reply brief was due. (See CCP sec. 1005(b).) Even considering the late-filed opposition, the demurrer is sustained on the merits. As to the first cause of action for breach of contract, the Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") does not plead facts to establish that Plaintiff was a party to the contract. As to the second cause of action for breach of contract, the SAC does not plead facts to establish that Plaintiff was a party to the contract. Additionally, the SAC does not plead facts that establish that the contract was expressly entered into for the benefit of Plaintiff; thus, Plaintiff cannot enforce the contract under a third party beneficiary theory. (See Shutes v. Cheney (1954) 123 Cal.App.2d 256, 262.) Further, Plaintiff provides no legal support for his assertion in his opposition brief that "[a]s a dues paying homeowner, Plaintiff is entitled to enforce the General Contract'" (Opposition, p. 3.) As to the third cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty, the SAC does not plead facts that establish Defendants owed a fiduciary duty to Plaintiffs. As to the fourth cause of action for trespass to real property, the SAC does not plead facts that establish that Defendants entered property possessed or controlled by Plaintiff. (See Civic Western Corp. v. Zila Industries, Inc. (1977) 66 Cal.App.3d 1, 16, "The essence of the cause of action for trespass is an 'unauthorized entry' onto the land of another.") The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend in light of the fact that Plaintiff has already had two opportunities to amend the complaint to state a cause of action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ANDREW SMITH, PLAINTIFF IN PRO PER.
SHARON FUTERMAN, ESQ., 916.443.4849, FOR DEFENDANTS THE OPERA PLAZA RESIDENTIAL PARCEL HOMEOWNERS and THE OPERA PLAZA MASTER OWNER'S ASSOCIATION.
UNIDENTIFIED COUNSEL APPEARING FOR UNKNOWN PARTY (COUNSEL DID NOT CHECK IN WITH CLERK AND PROVIDE BUSINESS CARD BEFORE CASE CALLED).

RULING: THE COURT DID NOT ISSUE A TENTATIVE RULING THE EVENING BEFORE TODAY'S HEARING. BEFORE ARGUMENT COMMENCED, THE COURT READ A TENTATIVE RULING ON THE RECORD AND PROVIDED COUNSEL (PRESENT IN COURT) WITH A COPY OF SAID RULING. MATTER ARGUED. AT CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT issues ruling regarding Defendants The Opera Plaza Residential Homeowner's Association's ("HOA") and The Opera Plaza Master Owner's Association' ("MOA") demurrer to Plaintiff's complaint, as follows: The demurrers to the first and second causes of action for breach of contract are sustained, with 20 days leave to amend, if Plaintiff can do so in good faith, to allege the specific terms of the contract(s) that each of these defendants (HOA and MOA) allegedly breached and how HOA and MOA breached the contracts. The demurrer to the third cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty is overruled. The demurrer to the fourth cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress is sustained with 20 days leave to amend, if Plaintiff can do in good faith, to allege facts showing that Defendants HOA and MOA engaged in conduct that was extreme and outrageous. The demurrer to the fifth cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress is sustained with 20 days leave to amend, if Plaintiff can do in good faith, to allege facts that Defendants HOA and MOA engaged in conduct that caused severe emotional distress. The demurrer to the sixth cause of action for trespass is sustained with 20 days leave to amend, if Plaintiff can do so in good faith, to allege facts that Defendants HOA and MOA engaged in acts that intentionally interfered with Plaintiff's property. The demurrer to the seventh cause of action for conversion is sustained with 20 days leave to amend, if Plaintiff can do so in good faith, to allege facts that Defendants HOA and MOA interfered with Plaintiff's personal property. The demurrer to the eighth cause of action is overruled as Plaintiff has sufficiently alleged the elements for a cause of action for intentional misrepresentation against HOA and MOA. The demurrer to the ninth cause of action for negligence is overruled as Plaintiff has sufficiently alleged facts to state a claim for negligence HOA and MOA. The demurrer to the tenth cause of action is sustained without leave to amend as California does not recognize a cause of action for unjust enrichment. The demurrer to the eleventh cause of action is overruled as Plaintiff has sufficiently pled a cause of action for equitable estoppel. The demurrer to the twelfth cause of action is sustained without leave to amend, since Plaintiff states in opposition to the demurrer that the count is directed to defendants other than HOA and MOA. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
